scrutiny_1_useSubmit_debug_ignore.finding: No c8 ignore directive exists in useSubmit.ts as delivered. The debug branch IS covered by 4 T-38 tests written by dev in the same PR. useSubmit.ts coverage: 100/100/100/100. No action needed from dev on this file.
scrutiny_1_useSubmit_debug_ignore.note: Sage's briefing described a c8 ignore that was apparently already removed or never committed to the working tree. Branch is genuinely covered.

scrutiny_2_keys_coverage_theater.finding: keys.ts has /* c8 ignore start */ through /* c8 ignore stop */ wrapping the ENTIRE file, masking all lines and branches. Coverage shows 100/100/100/100 but this is denominator theater — all lines excluded, not covered. The manufactured App.tsx import (const _releasePublicKey = getReleasePublicKey()) is a production call that exists purely to satisfy coverage optics. Per M1-implementation-spec.md Section 8, keys.ts is validated by the ADR-0005 OQ-3 shell gate test (bun build --define), NOT by Vitest.
scrutiny_2_keys_coverage_theater.required_dev_fix: Remove const _releasePublicKey = getReleasePublicKey() from App.tsx (line 17) — this call has no M1 purpose and must not ship in the binary for coverage reasons.
scrutiny_2_keys_coverage_theater.required_config_fix: Add src/security/keys.ts (or keys.ts glob) to vitest.config.ts coverage.exclude. keys.ts is build-define validated, not unit-testable under Node/Vitest. The c8 ignore start/stop must also be removed from keys.ts once the file is excluded from coverage.
scrutiny_2_keys_coverage_theater.routing: dev removes App.tsx manufactured import; staff-eng or dev updates vitest.config.ts exclude list; dev removes c8 ignore from keys.ts

scrutiny_3_token_issuance_useRef.finding: Dev's reasoning is correct. React ErrorBoundary.getDerivedStateFromError only catches errors thrown DURING render, not inside useEffect callbacks. Issuing the token in useEffect would mean a throw escapes the ErrorBoundary entirely. The useRef-during-render pattern is the correct fix for T-34. The once-only guard (tokenRef.current !== null check) is verified by T-37 which calls rerender() 2 additional times and asserts issueIdentityToken called exactly once (passes). Flag for staff-eng to bless and spec update to reflect the useRef deviation from the useEffect spec text.

ignore_directives_audit.keys.ts: BLOCK — c8 ignore start/stop masks entire file; theater coverage

coverage.note: keys.ts 100% is fake. Once exclude is added to vitest.config.ts, the real global coverage is 99.69% on the remaining files, which still passes.
